title: Classification of Systems
summary: This page introduces the classification of systems into eight broad categories including linear/nonlinear, causal/noncausal, and stable/unstable systems. It then begins discussing linear systems, explaining that linearity requires both the additivity property (superposition of inputs yields superposition of outputs) and the homogeneity/scaling property. The additivity property is expressed mathematically showing that if x1 produces y1 and x2 produces y2, then x1+x2 produces y1+y2.
